text: Quarterly Operations Policy All teams should submit a status update every Friday before 17:00. The security review for payment services must be completed before release. Each action item should have one owner and one due date. Follow-up actions should be reviewed in the next weekly planning meeting.
Team weekly memo. Alice should prepare KPI dashboard by Friday. Bob must review API security checklist before release. Carol should send customer summary next Monday.

Từ điển STUDY4 Tài khoản luyện thi Chương trình học Đề thi online Flashcards Blog Kích hoạt tài khoản sercetexam9 Flashcards: 900 từ IELTS (có ảnh) Xem ngẫu nhiên Dừng học list từ này 899 Tổng số từ 6 Đã học 6 Đã nhớ 0 Cần ôn tập List có 899 từ abate (verb) /əˈbeɪt/ Định nghĩa: (làm) dịu đi/yếu đi/giảm bớt Ví dụ: The storm showed no signs of abating. abhor (verb) /əbˈhɔːr/ Định nghĩa: ghê tởm; ghét cay ghét đắng Ví dụ: I abhor all forms of racism. abolish (verb) /əˈbɒlɪʃ/ Định nghĩa: bãi bỏ, xóa bỏ (luật, hệ thống, tổ chức) Ví dụ: This tax should be abolished. absorb (verb) /əbˈzɔːb/ Định nghĩa: hút, hấp thu Ví dụ: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air. abstract (noun) /ˈæbstrækt/ Định nghĩa: bài tóm lược (cuốn sách, luận án, bài diễn văn...) Ví dụ: An abstract always appears at the beginning of a manuscript or typescript. access (verb) /ˈækses/ Định nghĩa: tiếp cận, sử dụng Ví dụ: The loft can be accessed by a ladder. acclimatize (verb) /əˈklaɪmətaɪz/ Định nghĩa: thích nghi với môi trường, với khí hậu Ví dụ: Arrive two days early in order to acclimatize. accommodate (verb) /əˈkɒmədeɪt/ Định nghĩa: có chỗ cho, chứa được Ví dụ: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests. accomplished (adjective) /əˈkʌmplɪʃt/ Định nghĩa: có tài, tài năng Ví dụ: He was an accomplished artist by the age of 23. accuracy (noun) /ˈækjərəsi/ Định nghĩa: sự chính xác Ví dụ: They questioned the accuracy of the information in the file. accused (noun) /əˈkjuːzd/ Định nghĩa: bị cáo Ví dụ: The accused was found innocent. achievable (adjective) /əˈtʃiːvəbl̩/ Định nghĩa: có thể đạt được/thực hiện được Ví dụ: Profits of $20m look achievable. acquiesce (verb) /ˌækwiˈes/ Định nghĩa: bằng lòng, ưng thuận, đồng ý; bằng lòng ngầm, mặc nhận Ví dụ: Senior government figures must have acquiesced in the cover-up. acquire (verb) /əˈkwaɪər/ Định nghĩa: thu được, đạt được Ví dụ: He acquired the firm in 2008. activate (verb) /ˈæktɪveɪt/ Định nghĩa: kích hoạt Ví dụ: The burglar alarm is activated by movement. acute (adjective) /əˈkjuːt/ Định nghĩa: nghiêm trọng, nhức nhối (cơn đau) Ví dụ: There is an acute shortage of water. adapt (verb) /əˈdæpt/ Định nghĩa: thích nghi (với môi trường...) Ví dụ: It's amazing how soon you adapt. addicted (adjective) /əˈdɪktɪd/ Định nghĩa: nghiện (ma túy, cờ bạc,...) Ví dụ: to become addicted to drugs/gambling address (verb) /əˈdres/ Định nghĩa: chú trọng thảo luận/suy nghỉ về giải pháp cho vấn đề gì Ví dụ: We must address ourselves to the problem of both material and spiritual civilization. adequate (adjective) /ˈædɪkwət/ Định nghĩa: đủ, đầy đủ Ví dụ: We have an adequate supply of firewood to last the winter. adhere (verb) /ədˈhɪə(r)/ Định nghĩa: tuân theo Ví dụ: For ten months he adhered to a strict no-fat low-salt diet. adjust (verb) /əˈdʒʌst/ Định nghĩa: điều chỉnh, làm cho thích hợp Ví dụ: They'll be fine—they just need time to

have an adequate supply of firewood to last the winter. adhere (verb) /ədˈhɪə(r)/ Định nghĩa: tuân theo Ví dụ: For ten months he adhered to a strict no-fat low-salt diet. adjust (verb) /əˈdʒʌst/ Định nghĩa: điều chỉnh, làm cho thích hợp Ví dụ: They'll be fine—they just need time to adjust. admonish (verb) /ədˈmɒnɪʃ/ Định nghĩa: khiển trách, la rầy Ví dụ: She was admonished for chewing gum in class. adolescent (noun) /ˌædəlˈesənt/ Định nghĩa: thanh niên Ví dụ: Adolescents nowadays have problems that are different from ours. adopt (verb) /əˈdɒpt/ Định nghĩa: áp dụng (một phương pháp, một cách nhìn mới, ...) Ví dụ: The new tax would force companies to adopt energy-saving measures. adulthood (noun) /ˈædʌlthʊd/ Định nghĩa: tuổi khôn lớn, tuổi trưởng thành Ví dụ: a child reaching adulthood advantageous (adjective) /ˌædvənˈteɪdʒəs/ Định nghĩa: có lợi, thuận lợi Ví dụ: A free trade agreement would be advantageous to both countries. adventurous (adjective) /ədˈventʃərəs/ Định nghĩa: (thích) phiêu lưu, mạo hiểm Ví dụ: For the more adventurous tourists, there are trips into the mountains with a local guide. adversely (adverb) /ˈædvɜːsli/ Định nghĩa: xấu, tiêu cực Ví dụ: Her health was adversely affected by the climate. affable (adjective) /ˈæfəbl/ Định nghĩa: lịch sự, hoà nhã; niềm nở, ân cần Ví dụ: He struck me as an affable sort of a man. 1 2 3 4 5 © Về STUDY4 Giới thiệu Liên hệ Điều khoản bảo mật Điều khoản sử dụng Tài nguyên Thư viện đề thi Blog Tổng hợp tài liệu Chính sách chung Hướng dẫn sử dụng Hướng dẫn thanh toán Điều khoản và Điều Kiện Giao Dịch Chính sách giá bán Chính sách kiểm hàng Chính sách giao, nhận hàng Phản hồi, khiếu nại Chính sách chuyển đổi, hoàn hủy Thông tin doanh nghiệp CÔNG TY TNHH CÔNG NGHỆ A PLUS Điện thoại liên hệ/Hotline: 096 369 5525 Email: study4.team@gmail.com Địa chỉ trụ sở: Số 15, Ngõ 208 Giải Phóng, Phường Phương Liệt, Quận Thanh Xuân, Thành phố Hà Nội, Việt Nam Giấy chứng nhận Đăng ký doanh nghiệp số: 0109675459 do Sở Kế hoạch và Đầu tư thành phố Hà Nội cấp Ngày cấp phép: 17/06/2021 CS1: TRUNG TÂM NGOẠI NGỮ STUDY4 - Số 17, Ngõ 208 Giải Phóng, Phường Phương Liệt, Quận Thanh Xuân, Thành phố Hà Nội, Việt Nam - Quyết định cho phép Trung tâm ngoại ngữ hoạt động số 2654/QĐ-SGDĐT Hà Nội STUDY4.COM © Bản quyền của Công ty TNHH Công Nghệ A Plus. WRITING I. Bar Charts ● Purpose: To compare categories using bars. The _______________ provides about the percentages of divorces in Finland and Sweden between 2011 and 2015. ____________ , Sweden experienced a downward trend, while Finland showed an ____________ trend throughout the period. Both countries’ divorce rates had some fluctuations. Although Finland initially had a lower rate, it ____________ Sweden at the end of the period. Sweden’s divorce rate was about 45% in 2011, being higher than Finland’s rate by approximately 8%. Then, it rose to ____________ fifty percent in 2012. However, the figure showed a gradual decrease to about 47% in 2013, and continued to decline steadily to the end of the period, reaching around 45% in 2014 and _____________ to about 37% in 2015. Percentage of divorces in Finland was ___________ than 40% in 2011, and it decreased in 2012, when about one third of marriages in Finland ended with a divorce (in ___________ to almost a half in Sweden). However, the figure experienced a steady growth during the next two years. It rose to approximately 39% in 2013, then increased by around 3% in 2014, and remained steady for the next year, outracing the rate of Sweden. ● Key Features: ○ Highest/lowest values. ○ Comparisons between categories. ○ Changes over time (if present). ● Example: ○ "The bar chart illustrates the number of students enrolled in various language courses. Notably, Spanish had the highest enrollment, with over 200 students, while Italian had the lowest, at approximately 50." ○ "The most significant increase in sales was seen in the month of July." ● Vital Vocabulary: ○ "Significantly higher/lower," "considerably more/less," "comparable to," "in contrast," "whereas," "the majority," "the minority," "peaked at," "bottomed out at." II. Line Graphs ● Purpose: To show trends and changes over time. The line graph compares the fast food consumption of teenagers in Australia between 1975 and 2000, a period of 25 years. Overall, the consumption of _________ declined over the period, whereas the amount of _____ and _______that were eaten increased. In 1975, the most popular fast food with Australian teenagers was fish and chips, being eaten __ _____ times a year. This was far higher than pizza and hamburgers, which were consumed approximately _____ times a year. However, apart from a brief rise again from 1980 to 1985, the consumption of fish and chips gradually declined over the 25 year timescale to finish at just _________. In sharp contrast to this, teenagers ate the other two fast foods at much higher levels. Pizza __________ increased gradually until it overtook the consumption of fish and chips in _______. It then levelled off from 1995 to 2000. The biggest rise was seen in hamburgers as the occasions they were eaten increased sharply throughout the 1970’s and 1990’s, exceeding that of fish and chips in 1985. It finished at the same level that fish and chips began, with consumption at 100 times a year. ● Key Features: ○ Trends (increase, decrease, fluctuation). ○ Peaks and

as the occasions they were eaten increased sharply throughout the 1970’s and 1990’s, exceeding that of fish and chips in 1985. It finished at the same level that fish and chips began, with consumption at 100 times a year. ● Key Features: ○ Trends (increase, decrease, fluctuation). ○ Peaks and troughs. ○ Overall patterns. ● Example: ○ "The line graph depicts the fluctuations in oil prices over a ten-year period. There was a sharp rise in 2015, followed by a gradual decline until 2018." ○ "Overall, the trend shows a general increase over the whole period." ● Vital Vocabulary: ○ "Increased/decreased steadily/gradually/sharply/dramatically," "fluctuated," "remained stable/constant," "reached a peak/trough," "experienced a surge/decline," "subsequently," "initially," "eventually." III. Pie Charts ● Purpose: To represent proportions of a whole. The __________ detail the proportion of people’s total expenditure on ____ different goods and services (housing, clothing, entertainment, food, travel/transport, and luxury goods), in 1958 and 2008. It is clear from the charts that spending in all aspects, except _________, changed quite substantially over the fifty year period In 1958, people spent the largest portion of their spending on ______, at 32%. The two other categories that took up the majority of people’s money were________, at 22%, and _________ at 18%. Entertainment, travel/transport and luxury goods only comprised around a _________ of all spending at 13%, 8% and 7% respectively. 50 years later spending changed quite significantly with _________ now becoming the biggest expense and taking up almost __________ of the total spending, at 32%. The expenditure on clothing, transport/travel and luxury goods all increased and all took up around 16-17% of the _________. The expenditure on food dropped significantly to 12%, while entertainment costs were only half of that. ● Key Features: ○ Largest/smallest proportions. ○ Comparisons between slices. ○ Overall distribution. ● Example: ○ "The pie chart illustrates the distribution of household expenses. A significant proportion, 40%, was allocated to rent, while entertainment represented only 10%." ○ "Comprising the largest sector is the housing sector." ● Vital Vocabulary: ○ "A significant/substantial/considerable proportion," "a small/minor proportion," "representing," "accounting for," "comprising," "divided into," "the largest/smallest segment," "relative to." IV. Tables ● Purpose: To present data in rows and columns. ● Key Features: ○ Highest/lowest values. ○ Comparisons between categories. ○ Trends across rows/columns. ● Example: ○ "The table provides information on the population of three major cities. As can be seen, Tokyo has the highest population, with over 13 million residents, while London has approximately 9 million." ○ "In contrast, the figures for rural areas were significantly lower." ● Vital Vocabulary: ○ "As can be seen from the table," "in comparison," "similarly," "conversely," "respectively," "the data reveals," "the figures indicate," "the total number."
